title: 10 best herbal capsules for coughing fits
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for coughing fits are natural supplements that provide relief from persistent and bothersome coughs. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbs, such as marshmallow root, slippery elm, and licorice root, which help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause coughing fits. Examples include products like TheraCalm and CoughX, which have been shown to provide fast-acting relief from coughing fits and improve sleep quality. By providing natural relief, these capsules can greatly improve daily life for those suffering from chronic coughs, allowing them to breathe easier, sleep better, and enjoy improved overall health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with coughing fits because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, it soothes the throat and reduces inflammation caused by respiratory irritants, thereby relieving coughs and congestion. Additionally, licorice root has expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing fits. This natural remedy can provide fast relief from annoying and debilitating coughing episodes.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion in the throat and chest, which can contribute to coughing fits.

Liquiritin: This flavonoid has expectorant properties, meaning it helps loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion that can exacerbate coughs.

Licoric acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help combat underlying infections or irritants that may be causing coughing fits, such as colds, flu, or allergies.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help with coughing fits by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and exhibiting expectorant properties, which aid in the removal of mucus and phlegm.

Flavonoids (including Licorice Glycosides): Flavonoids, particularly licorice glycosides, help with coughing fits by providing anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and discomfort in the throat.
Coumarins: Coumarins help with coughing fits by exhibiting antitussive (cough-suppressing) properties, which can help to calm coughing spasms and provide relief from persistent coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that helps relieve coughs and soothe the throat.

Leaves: The leaves are used due to their secondary compound content, which also has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that aid in relieving coughs and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Barks: The barks are used due to their glycoside content, which helps to reduce inflammation and produce expectoration, making it easier to relieve coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier at a temperature range of 15-25 degrees celsius.
Grind 200 grams of the harvested roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder with a 1mm screen size.
Mix the powdered root with 100 grams of cornstarch and 20 grams of cellulose as a filler in a stainless steel container.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 grams of the root-cornstarch mixture using a capsule filling machine at a speed of 50 capsules per minute.
Label the capsules with the name of the product, ingredients, and dosage instructions in a clear and readable font using a label maker.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with coughing fits because they contain the active compounds found in the herb, such as thymol and carvacrol, which have natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. When taken orally, these compounds work to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation and congestion that can trigger coughs. Additionally, thyme's natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, allowing for easier breathing and relief from coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has antitussive properties that help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing coughing fits.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in thyme have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to persistent coughs.

Essential Oils: The essential oils contained in thyme capsules, such as borneol and terpinen-4-ol, have mucolytic properties that help break down and thin mucus in the airways, making it easier to expel and reducing coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol, a monoterpene, helps to reduce coughing fits by exhibiting antitussive properties, which inhibit the cough reflex and provide relief from coughing.

Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating coughing fits caused by inflammation.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, possesses antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat respiratory infections, such as the common cold, which can lead to coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antiseptic and expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughs and soothe the respiratory system.
Flowers: Employed for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and combat infections causing coughs.
Buds: Utilized for their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which help to relax the airways and promote the removal of mucus, relieving coughs and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers from a trusted source.
Clean and dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Fill 100-count gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the thymus vulgaris powder.
Label and store the herbal capsules in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena capsules helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties. The herb contains volatile oils that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel during a coughing fit. Additionally, lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the throat and bronchial tubes, providing relief from irritated tissues. This natural combination of actions helps alleviate coughing spasms and eases congestion, promoting a more restful night's sleep and improved overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Lemon verbena capsules contain compounds like citral and citronellal, which have been shown to reduce cough reflexes and alleviate coughing fits by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions.

Expectorant properties: The menthol content in lemon verbena helps thin mucus and promote its expulsion from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion and discomfort associated with coughing fits.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to chronic coughing, and also protect against oxidative stress caused by environmental pollutants and other irritants.
remedy_constituents: Verbascoside: A phenylethanoid glycoside that has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate coughing fits.

Luteolin: A flavonoid that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, leading to relief from coughing fits.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic acid that has anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughs and congestion.
Roots: The roots are used for their medicinal properties, including their ability to soothe coughs and reduce inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughs and congestion by promoting the clearance of mucus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 verbena officinalis flowers and leaves in the early morning to avoid moisture loss.
Dry the harvested verbena officinalis flowers and leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks.
Grind 2-3 tablespoons of dried verbena officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered verbena officinalis with 2-3 tablespoons of gelatin and 2-3 tablespoons of water to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of the verbena officinalis mixture and seal the capsules properly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with coughing fits because they soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The mucilage extracted from the marshmallow plant forms a protective film that shields the throat lining, making it easier to expel mucus and relieving persistent coughs. Additionally, marshmallow capsules can help thin out phlegm, allowing for a more productive cough, and providing rapid relief from coughing fits. This natural remedy is particularly beneficial for those suffering from chronic bronchitis, asthma, or allergies.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective mucilage that forms a soothing film on the mucous membranes, helping to calm and quiet coughs by reducing inflammation and irritation.

Antioxidants: Herbal marshmallow capsules contain antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to inflammation and damage in the throat and lungs, exacerbating coughing fits.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and coughing fits.
Gallic acid (a phenolic compound): Gallic acid exhibits anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties, helping to reduce coughing fits and alleviate respiratory discomfort.

Althaea root polysaccharides (arabinogalactan): Althaea root polysaccharides have been shown to have a soothing effect on the throat, reducing inflammation and coughing fits, and promoting expectoration of mucus.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in mucilages, a type of soluble fiber that helps soothe and calm irritated tissues in the throat.
Leaves: They contain a high concentration of mucilages, similar to the roots, which makes them effective in reducing coughing fits.
Stems: While not as commonly used as roots and leaves, the stems of Althaea officinalis also contain mucilages that can help alleviate coughing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with a natural capsule filler such as rice flour in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the root mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with coughing fits because of their natural ability to soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The menthol content in peppermint oil has a natural expectorant property, which helps to thin and clear mucus from the airways, providing instant relief from congestion and coughs. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling in the throat and chest, making it easier to breathe and alleviating discomfort associated with persistent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the throat and lungs, which helps to alleviate coughing fits caused by inflammation or irritation.
Expectorant properties: The menthol content in peppermint capsules helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion that can trigger coughing.

Relaxant properties: Peppermint's natural oils have a calming effect on the smooth muscles of the respiratory tract, which relaxes the airways and reduces spasms that can lead to persistent coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and promote the clearance of mucus, thereby alleviating coughing fits.
Menthol: A terpene with a cooling and decongestant effect, menthol helps to thin mucus and ease its expulsion from the respiratory tract, providing relief from coughing and congestion.
Menthone: A terpene with expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, menthone helps to break down and clear mucus from the airways, reducing coughing and promoting a smoother breathing experience.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of menthol and menthone, which have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Although not as commonly used as leaves, peppermint stems may be used for their menthol and menthone content, similar to the leaves.
Buds: Peppermint buds may be used due to their high oil content and menthol properties, which can help to soothe a sore throat and alleviate coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the morning when they are at their peak oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 30-40 degrees celsius for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind 5 grams of dried mentha x piperita leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 5 grams of cellulose or other natural filler material in a small bowl.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to maintain their potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with coughing fits because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated airways. The capsules contain a concentrated extract of Eucalyptus globulus, a plant native to Tasmania known for its decongestant and expectorant qualities. When taken internally, the Tasmanian blue gum helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, providing fast relief from stubborn coughs and congestion. As a result, individuals can find much-needed comfort and peace of mind as they breathe easy once again.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: A natural decongestant that helps relieve respiratory congestion and reduce mucus production, alleviating cough symptoms.
Cineole: An expectorant that helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and reducing coughing fits.
Menthol: A natural anesthetic that numbs the mucous membranes in the throat, providing quick relief from coughing spasms and irritation.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: It helps with coughing fits by acting as a natural expectorant, loosening and thinning mucus in the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up.
Cineole: It aids in relieving coughs by reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways, allowing for smoother breathing and alleviation of coughing fits.
α-Pinene: This constituent helps by reducing inflammation and opening airways, thereby decreasing the frequency and severity of coughing fits and promoting easier breathing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Eucalyptus globulus are primarily used due to their high eucalyptol content, which provides expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve coughs.
Barks: The barks of Eucalyptus globulus are commonly used due to their high concentration of volatile oils, which help to ease congestion and reduce coughing.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus are occasionally used, often in combination with other parts, to provide additional eucalyptol content and help alleviate coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in late summer for optimal potency and flavor.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 99 grams of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a powder scoop and capsule filling machine for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to maintain their potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue flag capsules helps with coughing fits because they contain the herb Iris versicolor, which has been traditionally used to soothe irritated respiratory tissues. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat, while their expectorant properties loosen and clear out excess mucus, making it easier for the body to expel. This combination of actions helps to alleviate coughing fits and provides relief from congestion and chest tightness associated with colds, flu, and allergies.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Blue Flag's saponins help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and coughs.
Anti-inflammatory property: The plant's flavonoids and alkaloids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate coughing fits.

Antiviral property: Blue Flag's bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain respiratory viruses, helping to combat infections that contribute to persistent coughing.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help relieve coughing fits by reducing inflammation in the airways and promoting the clearance of mucus.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate coughing fits by reducing the severity of respiratory infections and inflammation.

Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate coughing fits by reducing inflammation in the airways and promoting the healing of damaged tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Iris versicolor are used to make capsules for coughing fits due to their expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and coughs.
Leaves: The leaves of Iris versicolor are used to make capsules for coughing fits due to their mucilaginous properties, soothing the throat and reducing inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of Iris versicolor are used to make capsules for coughing fits due to their expectorant properties and ability to thin mucus, making it easier to expel.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried iris versicolor flowers and leaves from a trusted source by the full moon.
Dry the iris versicolor flowers and leaves at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours using a temperature-controlled dryer.
Grind the dried iris versicolor into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the iris versicolor powder with 1 gram of cellulose and 1 gram of gelatin to create a capsule mixture.
Encapsulate the mixture into 100 capsules using a manual capsule machine with a capacity of 1 gram per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Selfheal capsules helps with coughing fits because they contain the herb Prunella vulgaris, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and airways. This relief from inflammation allows for easier breathing and reduced irritation, helping to ease the discomfort and frequency of coughing fits. Additionally, selfheal's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, further reducing the severity of coughs and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Selfheal's antibacterial compounds, such as prunellin and rosmarinic acid, help to combat bacterial infections in the respiratory tract that can cause or worsen coughs.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Selfheal's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs and airways, which can help alleviate symptoms of coughing fits.

Expectorant properties: Selfheal's expectorant compounds, such as mucilage and saponins, can help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and reducing the frequency and severity of coughs.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, relieving coughing fits.
Flavonoids: They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help calm the respiratory system, reducing coughing and congestion.
Saponins: They exhibit expectorant properties, helping to thin mucus and promote its expulsion from the respiratory tract, thus alleviating coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Prunella vulgaris are used to make capsules for coughing fits due to their expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and coughs.
Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce coughing.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their antitussive properties, which can help relieve coughing fits by suppressing the cough reflex.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of fresh prunella vulgaris leaves and flowers at the peak of their potency in summer.
Dry the collected material at 40°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture and preserve the herb's properties.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind 10 grams of the dried prunella vulgaris into a fine powder.
Mix 5 grams of the powdered prunella vulgaris with 5 grams of gelatin in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill 10-20 empty gelatin capsules with the prunella vulgaris-gelatin mixture and seal them using a capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain capsules helps with coughing fits because they contain the medicinal properties of the plantain leaf, which has been traditionally used to soothe irritated throats and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds in the capsules work to calm the mucous membranes, reducing the severity and frequency of coughs. Additionally, the plantain's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from congestion and discomfort associated with chronic coughing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Plantain contains anti-inflammatory compounds like allantoin and mucilage, which help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, soothing the discomfort associated with coughing.

Expectorant properties: The mucilages present in Plantain capsules can help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and bronchi, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieving congestion-related coughs.

Antioxidants: Plantain's antioxidant content helps protect the respiratory tract from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate coughing fits and other symptoms of upper respiratory infections.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, alleviating coughing fits by soothing irritated tissues.
Apolipoprotein A-1: Stimulates the production of surfactant, a substance that reduces inflammation and irritation in the lungs, thereby easing coughing fits.
Apolipoprotein A-V: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help calm the respiratory system and reduce the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules for coughing fits due to their mucilaginous properties, which help soothe irritated throats and coughs.
Seeds: Used due to their expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, relieving coughs.
Roots: Used for their antitussive properties, which help reduce the frequency and severity of coughs by suppressing cough receptors in the brain.
remedy_recipe: Dry the plantago major leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried plantago major leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered plantago major leaves with 1 tablespoon of gelatin in a bowl to create a gelatinous mixture.
Add a small amount of distilled water to the gelatinous mixture to create a thick paste that can be filled into capsules.
Fill the capsules with the thick paste using a capsule filling machine and seal the capsules tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with coughing fits because they possess natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs. The capsule's extract, rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, acts as a powerful antioxidant that reduces inflammation and calms down the cough reflex, providing rapid relief from persistent coughs. Additionally, dandelion's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier for the body to expel, thus reducing coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate coughing fits and make breathing easier.
Mucus thinning and expulsion: The taraxasterol present in dandelion capsules has been shown to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and coughing.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Dandelion's antiviral and antibacterial properties can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to coughing fits, such as the common cold or bronchitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate coughing fits.
Taraxasterol: This triterpene has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughing fits by loosening and expelling mucus from the respiratory tract.

Phenolic acids (specifically, chlorogenic acid): These compounds have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat underlying infections and reduce the severity of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve coughing fits.
Leaves: They are used because they contain compounds that help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation associated with coughs.
Seeds: They are used for their expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Dry the taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in a single layer at 50 degrees celsius for 8 hours.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of the taraxacum officinale powder with 250 milligrams of a filler agent like potato starch.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the taraxacum officinale mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the taraxacum officinale capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with coughing fits because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken in capsule form, goldenseal can help to reduce the severity of coughs caused by respiratory infections such as bronchitis, pneumonia, or sinusitis. Berberine works to combat invading bacteria, reducing congestion and inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate dry, hacking coughs and provide fast relief from fitful coughing episodes.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has antibacterial properties, helping to combat infections that can cause coughs and reduce inflammation in the throat.
Isoquinoline Alkaloids (IAs): Have anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues, reducing coughing fits.
Berberastatin: Has antioxidant properties, helping to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by chronic coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a natural alkaloid that has antitussive and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving coughing fits.
Rhyzomes: Contain berberine and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion associated with coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried hydrastis canadensis root powder weighing approximately 250 milligrams.
Mix the dried hydrastis canadensis root powder with gelatin capsules in a ratio of one part powder to two parts capsule.
Fill size zero empty hard shell capsules with the powdered mixture and seal each capsule individually.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months prior to use.
Administer three to four capsules thrice daily as needed to alleviate coughing fits, allowing for optimal absorption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava capsules helps with coughing fits because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work together to relax the respiratory muscles, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion. The kavalactones in kava capsules have natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the airways and calm the cough reflex, providing fast relief from persistent coughs and hacking fits. Additionally, kava's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress-related coughing, allowing you to breathe easier and sleep better at night.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: These compounds have been shown to have potent antitussive (cough-suppressing) effects by interacting with the brain's reward system and reducing the urge to cough.
Antioxidant properties: Kava's antioxidants may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to chronic coughing and make it more challenging to manage.

Muscle relaxant properties: The kavalactones in kava capsules have been found to possess muscle-relaxing effects, which can help alleviate spasms in the chest muscles that often accompany persistent coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in kavalactones, which have been shown to effectively reduce cough and soothe irritated throats.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of calcium, which helps to relax bronchial muscles and alleviate coughing spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried kava root and 1 ounce of dried ginger root for the herbal blend.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2000 milligrams of the herbal blend with 1000 milligrams of glycerin and 500 milligrams of silicon dioxide.
Fill #00 capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them immediately after filling to preserve potency.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or longer if refrigerated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with coughing fits because they contain extracts of Passiflora incarnata, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to soothe respiratory issues. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate coughs and make them more difficult to manage. Additionally, maypop capsules' expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, allowing for easier breathing and a more effective cough response.
remedy_properties: Expectorant: Maypop capsules contain papain, an enzyme that helps to break down mucus, making it easier to expel, thus relieving congestion and coughing fits.
Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids and alkaloids present in maypop have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, alleviating coughing and soreness.
Antispasmodic: Maypop's constituents, such as passionarin, have a calming effect on the respiratory tract, helping to relax airway muscles and reducing spasms that can cause persistent coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe irritated airways and reduce inflammation in the lungs, making them an effective ingredient in coughing fit medication.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the Maypop plant are rich in mucilage, a natural expectorant that helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief for coughs and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves.
Dry the plant material in a warm, dry place or using a dehydrator.
Crush the dried plant material into small pieces using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the crushed plant material with a filler such as potato starch or silica gel.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock capsules helps with coughing fits because they contain the herb Rumex crispus, which has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated throats. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating the discomfort associated with coughing fits. Additionally, the capsules' expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, providing relief from stubborn coughs.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Curly dock contains saponins, which have been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, thus relieving congestion and reducing coughing fits.

Tannins: Tannins in curly dock capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, easing discomfort and reducing the frequency of coughing episodes.

Phenolic acids: Curly dock's phenolic acid content has been found to possess antibacterial properties, helping to combat infections that can cause chronic coughing fits by suppressing the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus pneumoniae.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages and saponins, which help soothe and calm irritated respiratory tract tissues, providing relief from coughing fits.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and congestion in the lungs, alleviating coughing spasms.
remedy_recipe: Dry fresh rumex crispus leaves in a warm oven at 35°c for two hours.
Grind dried rumex crispus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 250 milligrams of ground rumex crispus powder with 500 milliliters of distilled water to create a tea infusion.
Steep the mixture in a saucepan at 80°c for 10 minutes to allow flavors and nutrients to combine.
Fill gelatin capsules with the cooled tea infusion mixture and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with coughing fits because of their natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb yarrow, also known as Achillea millefolium, contains a compound called achillicin, which soothes the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by coughs. As the capsules dissolve in the body, they help to break down mucus and phlegm, making it easier to expel and alleviate the discomfort of persistent coughing fits. Additionally, yarrow's antimicrobial properties help to combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses, promoting a healthy respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow contains anti-inflammatory compounds like acetic acid, which help reduce inflammation in the lungs and throat, alleviating coughs caused by respiratory infections.
Expectorant properties: Yarrow's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
Antiviral compounds: Yarrow contains antiviral compounds like flavonoids, which can help combat viral infections that cause coughs, such as the common cold and flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: Yarrow roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and soothing a hacking cough.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers contain flavonoids and volatile oils that help to reduce congestion and ease coughing by loosening phlegm and relieving bronchial spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh leaves of achillea millefolium and dry them in a warm place for at least two weeks.
Weigh out 20 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves and mix with 10 grams of silica gel to prevent moisture absorption.
Load the mixture into an industrial capsule machine and fill capsules according to manufacturer's instructions with approximately 250-milligram dosage per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for at least one week before use to allow for proper curing process.
Take two capsules, three times a day, with water or other liquid of choice, for up to six weeks to alleviate persistent coughing fits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with coughing fits because they contain the soothing properties of Lavandula angustifolia, a natural expectorant that eases congestion and calms irritated airways. The antispasmodic effects of lavender help to reduce muscle spasms in the throat and chest, alleviating intense coughing fits and making breathing easier. Additionally, lavender's calming influence can promote relaxation, helping to reduce stress-induced coughing and promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lavender's antispasmodic effects help to relax and soothe the respiratory tract, reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing fits.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to chronic coughs and make them more manageable.

Anxiolytic properties: Lavender's anxiolytic effects can help to calm the nervous system and reduce stress, which is often a trigger for coughing fits, allowing the body to better cope with respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, lavender roots have been traditionally used to soothe respiratory issues and calm coughing fits.
Seeds: Containing a unique combination of fatty acids and terpenes, lavender seeds are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and ease congestion associated with coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and leaves.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 24 hours.
Combine 200mg of dried lavender flowers and leaves with 100mg of pure beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160°f for 20 minutes to create a smooth infusion blend.
Fill clean empty gelatin capsules with the cooled lavender-beeswax infusion and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with coughing fits because they contain the natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties of elderberry. The berries, leaves, and flowers of the elder plant have been traditionally used to soothe respiratory issues such as coughs, colds, and flu. When consumed as a capsule, the compounds in elderberry help to calm irritated airways, reduce inflammation, and ease the constriction of bronchial tubes, providing relief from persistent coughing fits. As a result, elder capsules can offer natural and effective relief for individuals suffering from chronic coughing issues.
remedy_properties: Sambucine: This alkaloid helps to relax the muscles in the respiratory tract, reducing spasms and relieving persistent coughs and wheezing.
Quercetin: As a natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory, quercetin helps to reduce inflammation in the lungs and throat, which can exacerbate coughing fits.
Rutoside: This flavonoid has mucilage properties, providing a soothing coating for the irritated respiratory tract and reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the elder plant contain compounds that help soothe and calm coughs, making them a popular choice for medicinal capsules.
Barks: The barks of the elder plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the throat and alleviate cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves from a trusted source, ensuring freshness and quality.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for at least 24 hours or until crispy.
Crush the dried sambucus nigra into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered sambucus nigra with 500 milliliters of water to create an infusion, steeping for 8-10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the infused liquid mixture, sealing and labeling them properly for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with coughing fits because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, gingerol, soothes the inflamed airways and mucous membranes, reducing irritation and inflammation that often triggers coughing. Additionally, ginger has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs, providing relief from stubborn coughs. As a result, taking herbal ginger capsules can provide quick and effective relief from persistent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughing fits.

Expectorant and mucolytic properties: The saponins and alkaloids present in ginger capsules help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and coughing fits by making it easier to expel phlegm.

Analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties: Ginger's analgesic and anti-spasmodic compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help relieve pain and spasms in the throat and chest, reducing discomfort and coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving coughs and congestion.
Roots: Contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are known for their ability to reduce inflammation and open up airways, providing relief from persistent coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger roots weighing approximately 500 grams for processing.
Clean the harvested ginger roots by washing them thoroughly with cold running water and scrubbing off any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned ginger roots using a food dehydrator at a temperature of 40°c for 12 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind dried ginger into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle and sift through a fine-mesh sieve to ensure uniform texture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 200-250 milligrams of the ground ginger powder and seal immediately.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to coughing fits that are treated with herbal capsules are bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). Herbal capsules containing ingredients like licorice root, turmeric, and ginger can help soothe the respiratory tract, reduce inflammation, and ease congestion. Other herbs like Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Slippery Elm may also be used to treat these conditions, promoting a healthy cough response and relieving symptoms such as wheezing and shortness of breath.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with coughing fits is a blend of Thyme, Echinacea, and Slippery Elm. Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat underlying infections, while Echinacea stimulates the immune system to fight off invading pathogens. Slippery Elm, with its soothing mucilage, coats and calms the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort. This synergistic blend of herbs can help alleviate coughing fits, promote healing, and support overall respiratory health. Consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal supplements.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Alternative therapies in health and medicine, the study aimed to review the antitussive effects of various extracts, fractions, and constituents of medicinal plants. The researchers searched various databases for studies published between 1978 and 2015, using keywords such as antitussive and cough, and the names of various medicinal plants and their constituents. The results showed that Nigella sativa and Linum usitatissimum exhibited the most potent antitussive effects on coughs induced by sulfur dioxide, while Artemisia absinthium was more effective against ammonia-induced cough, and Cuminum cyminum and Glycyrrhiza glabra were more potent against citric acid-induced cough, suggesting that herbal capsules containing these plants could be beneficial in alleviating coughing fits.
